detail: d=4 bisection floor: B=8 >= sqrt(64)=8 PASS
d=6 bisection floor: B=15 >= sqrt(64)=8 PASS
d=4 tornado throughput: 0.155 <= ideal 0.250 PASS (62% allocator efficiency)
d=6 tornado throughput: 0.195 <= ideal 0.469 PASS (42% allocator efficiency)
d=4 zero-load latency: 64.70 >= floor 27.5 PASS (router pipeline ~37 cy)
d=6 zero-load latency: 57.40 >= floor 26.1 PASS (router pipeline ~31 cy)

verdict.rationale: At this single (4 GHz, 22 nm, axial-hex-on-square-grid placement) point, d=6 outperforms d=4 on both axes (0.887x zero-load latency, 1.26x saturation throughput) despite the 1.5x router port-cost and the 2-cycle diagonal-link penalty. This is NOT a regime claim. F1/F2 (degree-6 vs degree-4 at modern node) requires resolving all three rfc_001 §9 open questions before a PASS/FAIL verdict: (i) alternative hex placements (king-move d=8, optimized non-square layouts); (ii) clock-frequency sweep across 1-6 GHz including the integer-cycle crossings; (iii) wire-delay-number sensitivity across newer-FinFET-class characterizations. Pair flagged LEAN-PASS-FOR-D6 informally but verdict enum stays INCONCLUSIVE.

provenance.atlas_cite_block: @cite hexa-arch:rfc_001 §4 + §5 + §8; rfc_002 §3 pair-record convention; design.md D1 + D7
